steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: The phrase before the blank ends with “in the first half of the twentieth [blank],” which needs a noun to complete the first independent clause. The phrase after the blank—”these ingenious devices were eventually replaced by electronic calculators”—is a second independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the boundary rule
steps.content:
Choice C provides “century, but,” which joins the two clauses using the rule: a comma followed by a coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses.

The word “but” also expresses the semantic contrast between the devices' popularity and their replacement.

Choices A, B, and
  • D each violate this boundary rule: A has no connector
  • B has only a comma
  • D uses “that” as an inappropriate relative pronoun
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
